prs.title: Fix/improve playwright pools spec

labels.name: area:UI
labels.description: Related to UI/UX. For Frontend Developers.

prs.body:
Improve Playwright test patterns in `pools.spec.ts` and `PoolsPage.ts`
to align with Playwright Best Practices.

This PR focuses on improving test patterns only.
It does not change test coverage or behavior.

**Changes made:**
- `getPoolCard()`: replaced `.locator("div")` with `.locator("> div")` 
   for direct child scoping — avoids matching nested divs
- `verifyPoolSlots()`: split into two separate assertions (pool card 
   visibility + slot count text) instead of one brittle combined string match

closes: #63430

---

##### Was generative AI tooling used to co-author this PR?

If generative AI tooling has been used in the process of authoring this PR, please
change below checkbox to `[X]` followed by the name of the tool, uncomment the "Generated-by".
-->

- [ ] Yes (please specify the tool below)

<!--
Generated-by: [Tool Name] following [the guidelines](https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/main/contributing-docs/05_pull_requests.rst#gen-ai-assisted-contributions)
-->

---

* Read the **[Pull Request Guidelines](https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/main/contributing-docs/05_pull_requests.rst#pull-request-guidelines)** for more information. Note: commit author/co-author name and email in commits become permanently public when merged.
* For fundamental code changes, an Airflow Improvement Proposal ([AIP](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/AIRFLOW/Airflow+Improvement+Proposals)) is needed.
* When adding dependency, check compliance with the [ASF 3rd Party License Policy](https://www.apache.org/legal/resolved.html#category-x).
* For significant user-facing changes create newsfragment: `{pr_number}.significant.rst`, in [airflow-core/newsfragments](https://github.com/apache/airflow/tree/main/airflow-core/newsfragments). You can add this file in a follow-up commit after the PR is created so you know the PR number.

prs.title: Add json-user-spend-summary — Data Processing Harbor Task
prs.body: # Add `json-user-spend-summary` — Data Processing Harbor Task ## Overview This PR introduces a new Harbor task: **`json-user-spend-summary`**, a deterministic data-processing benchmark designed to evaluate an agent’s ability to: - Read structured JSON input - Perform aggregation logic - Sort results deterministically - Write formatted output to a required file The task is categorized as **Easy** under `data-processing`. --- ## 📂 Task Structure ``` harbor_tasks/json-user-spend-summary/ ├── task.toml ├── instruction.md ├── environment/ │ ├── Dockerfile │ └── input.json ├── solution/ │ └── solve.sh └── tests/ ├── test.sh └── test_outputs.py ``` --- ## Task Objective Given `/app/input.json`: ```json [ {"user": "alice", "amount": 100}, {"user": "bob", "amount": 200}, {"user": "alice", "amount": 50}, {"user": "charlie", "amount": 300} ] ``` The agent must: 1. Compute total spend per user 2. Sort users by total spend (descending) 3. Write results to `/app/output.txt` in the exact format: ``` charlie: 300 bob: 200 alice: 150 ``` --- ## 🛠 Design Decisions - Uses **absolute paths only** (`/app/...`) - Deterministic ordering to enable strict equality tests - No hardcoded output values - Inline Python used in `solve.sh` for robust JSON parsing - Explicit reward handling via `/logs/verifier/reward.txt` - Fully Docker-isolated execution - Canary GUID embedded in required files - Ruff linting passes with no issues --- ## Validation Results ### Oracle Agent - Mean: **1.000** - Trials: 1 - Errors: 0 📎 Screenshot attached below. --- ### NOP Agent - Mean: **0.000** - Trials: 1 - Errors: 0 📎 Screenshot attached below. --- ## ✔️ Success Criteria Met - Oracle test returns **1.0** - NOP test returns **0.0** - Ruff linting passes - Reward file generated correctly - Task does not auto-pass - Proper Docker isolation maintained --- Please let me know if any refinements or improvements are required. ---

prs.title: Add json-user-spend-summary: JSON aggregation data-processing task
prs.body: # Add `json-user-spend-summary` — Data Processing Harbor Task ## Overview This PR introduces a new Harbor task: **`json-user-spend-summary`**, a deterministic data-processing benchmark designed to evaluate an agent’s ability to: - Read structured JSON input - Perform aggregation logic - Sort results deterministically - Write formatted output to a required file The task is categorized as **Easy** under `data-processing`. --- ## 📂 Task Structure ``` harbor_tasks/json-user-spend-summary/ ├── task.toml ├── instruction.md ├── environment/ │ ├── Dockerfile │ └── input.json ├── solution/ │ └── solve.sh └── tests/ ├── test.sh └── test_outputs.py ``` --- ## Task Objective Given `/app/input.json`: ```json [ {"user": "alice", "amount": 100}, {"user": "bob", "amount": 200}, {"user": "alice", "amount": 50}, {"user": "charlie", "amount": 300} ] ``` The agent must: 1. Compute total spend per user 2. Sort users by total spend (descending) 3. Write results to `/app/output.txt` in the exact format: ``` charlie: 300 bob: 200 alice: 150 ``` --- ## 🛠 Design Decisions - Uses **absolute paths only** (`/app/...`) - Deterministic ordering to enable strict equality tests - No hardcoded output values - Inline Python used in `solve.sh` for robust JSON parsing - Explicit reward handling via `/logs/verifier/reward.txt` - Fully Docker-isolated execution - Canary GUID embedded in required files - Ruff linting passes with no issues --- ## Validation Results ### Oracle Agent - Mean: **1.000** - Trials: 1 - Errors: 0 📎 Screenshot attached below. --- ### NOP Agent - Mean: **0.000** - Trials: 1 - Errors: 0 📎 Screenshot attached below. --- ## ✔️ Success Criteria Met - Oracle test returns **1.0** - NOP test returns **0.0** - Ruff linting passes - Reward file generated correctly - Task does not auto-pass - Proper Docker isolation maintained --- Please let me know if any refinements or improvements are required. ---

prs.title: fix(bash): correct <...> tokenization in shellscript grammar so final character is highlighted consistently
prs.body:
### Description

This PR fixes incorrect bash `<...>` tokenization in the shellscript grammar, where the **final character before `>` was not highlighted** with the rest of the argument.

Example of broken behavior (current Shiki):

- `pfinfo <usual_guide>` → `e` is uncolored  
- `test <abcd>` → `d` is uncolored  

This happened because the upstream VS Code grammar uses a negative lookahead `(?!>)` inside the `argument_context` pattern, which excludes characters immediately preceding `>`.

#### What this PR does:

1. **Adds a lightweight grammar override system**  
   Allows Shiki to merge `packages/langs/src/overrides/<lang>.json` into the upstream TextMate grammar.  
   This avoids forking entire grammars and keeps Shiki compatible with upstream changes.

2. **Introduces a bash-specific override**  
   Replaces the problematic `argument_context` pattern without the `(?!>)` guard, ensuring the full `<...>` argument is matched consistently.

3. **Adds a regression test + snapshot**  
   `pfinfo <usual_guide>` and `test <abcd>` now tokenize correctly, keeping all characters inside `<...>` within the same scope.

All snapshots updated and passing.

### Linked Issues

Fixes #1020 (bash angle bracket highlighting)

### Additional context

The patch is intentionally minimal and layered through an override file rather than modifying the upstream VS Code grammar directly.  
This approach matches Shiki’s design of keeping grammars in sync with VS Code while still allowing targeted fixes when necessary.

Reviewers may want to look specifically at:

- The override merge logic in `packages/langs/scripts/langs.ts`
- The bash override file
- The new regression test in `packages/core/test/fixtures/bash-angle-brackets.test.ts`

Everything else is auto-generated from the build pipeline.

prs.title: fix: silentJSONParsing=false should throw on invalid JSON (#7253)
prs.body:
# Fix: silentJSONParsing=false should throw on invalid JSON (#7253)

## 🐛 Bug Fix: `silentJSONParsing = false` did not throw on invalid JSON

This PR fixes incorrect behavior in Axios where setting:

```js
transitional: {
  silentJSONParsing: false
}
```

**did not throw a SyntaxError** for malformed JSON unless `responseType: 'json'` was also explicitly set.

### ✔ Expected Behavior

When `silentJSONParsing: false`, Axios should throw on invalid JSON **always**, regardless of `responseType`.

### ❌ Actual Behavior

Axios ignored `silentJSONParsing: false` unless:

```js
responseType: 'json'
```

This resulted in swallowed JSON parsing errors, even when users explicitly opted out of silent parsing.

---

## ✅ What This PR Changes

### 1. **Fixes strictJSONParsing logic in `lib/defaults/index.js`**

#### Before:

```js
const strictJSONParsing = !silentJSONParsing && JSONRequested;
```

#### After:

```js
const strictJSONParsing = !silentJSONParsing;
```

Now, when a user sets `silentJSONParsing: false`, Axios correctly throws a `SyntaxError` whenever forced JSON parsing fails, even if `responseType` is not `'json'`.

---

## 🧪 Tests Added

A new regression test file has been added:

```
test/regression_silent_json.js
```

The tests cover:

* `silentJSONParsing = false` → should **throw**
* `silentJSONParsing = true` → should **not throw**
* Behavior with `responseType = 'json'`
* Transitional default behavior

All tests pass locally.

---

## 📦 Files Changed

```
lib/defaults/index.js
test/regression_silent_json.js
```

No unrelated files were modified.

---

## 💬 Maintainer Notes

* No API surface changes.
* Fix brings behavior in line with documentation and user expectations.
* Fully backward compatible for users relying on default transitional settings.

---

prs.title: fix: prevent noSmooth crash on p5.Graphics (resolves #8264)
prs.body:
Resolves #8264

## Changes:
This PR fixes a crash when calling `noSmooth()` on a `p5.Graphics` object in p5.js 2.0, which previously resulted in:


### What was done:
- Updated `src/shape/attributes.js` to safely route through  
  `renderer.setSmoothing(false)` when available.
- Added a fallback that manually disables  
  `drawingContext.imageSmoothingEnabled` for cases where `setSmoothing` or `setAttributes` do not exist.
- Ensures `noSmooth()` works consistently on:
  - Main canvas (2D)
  - p5.Graphics 2D buffers  
  - p5.Graphics WebGL buffers (the crash case)

### Manual test pages added:
- `test/manual/noSmooth/canvas-2d.html`
- `test/manual/noSmooth/graphics-webgl.html`

These test pages verify that `noSmooth()` runs without errors and logs success messages in the console.

---

## Screenshots of the change:
**Console output after fix:**

- Main Canvas (2D):  
  `Main Canvas: noSmooth OK`

- WebGL p5.Graphics:  
  `WebGL p5.Graphics: noSmooth OK`

No crashes, no TypeErrors.

---

#### PR Checklist

- [x] `npm run lint` passes  
- [x] [Inline reference] is included / updated  
- [x] [Unit tests] are included / updated (manual tests added for now)

[Inline reference]: https://p5js.org/contribute/contributing_to_the_p5js_reference/
[Unit tests]: https://github.com/processing/p5.js/tree/main/contributor_docs#unit-tests

fix(docs): ensure language switch links use target=_self (closes #21112)
prs.body:
This PR implements the expected behavior for language switching as described in Issue #21112.
What was happening
Language links (e.g., cn.vite.dev, ja.vite.dev, etc.) opened in a new tab, because they were treated as external links.

What this PR changes
Language subdomain links (xx.vite.dev) are now detected and forced to open with:
target="_self"
This ensures smoother navigation when switching documentation languages.

✔ Implementation details
•Added domain detection for localized Vite documentation sites
•Applied _self for links that match the pattern:
/^[a-z]{2}\.vite\.dev$/

Existing behavior is preserved:
External links → _blank

Internal links → no target attribute

prs.title: docs: fix language switcher opening links in a new tab (fix #21112)
prs.body:
This PR fixes the behavior of the language switcher in the documentation site.

🐛 Problem
Language links such as:
https://ja.vite.dev
https://zh-cn.vite.dev
https://ko.vite.dev
were being treated as external links by VitePress, causing them to render with:
target="_blank"
As a result, selecting a language opened the documentation in a new browser tab, which is not the expected UX for locale switching.

See issue: #21112
✅ Solution
This PR removes the target="_blank" attribute only for links that belong to:
•vite.dev
•*.vite.dev (locale subdomains)
A small enhancement is added inside docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts that:
•Runs only on the client (typeof window !== 'undefined')
•Scans for anchors with target="_blank"
•Parses URLs safely
•Removes the target only for vite.dev subdomains
•Uses a MutationObserver to handle VitePress client-side routing updates
This approach is safe, minimal, and does not affect actual external links.

🧪 Testing

•Verified by inspecting rendered anchor elements and confirming:
•Language links no longer have target="_blank"
•External links remain unchanged
Behavior persists after navigation (due to MutationObserver)

📁 Changed files
•docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts

✔️ Summary

Selecting a language now opens in the same tab, matching expected documentation UX.

Fixes: #21112

issues.title: Incorrect Status for Past Appointments + Missing Review Option
issues.body:
Fix Past Appointment Status and Add Review Feature

In the User Dashboard > Appointment History, past appointments are still marked as “Scheduled”, even though the date/time has already passed.

Also, there is no option for the user to leave a review, which should be available only after a completed appointment.

Expected Behavior:

If the appointment is in the past and was not canceled:

Mark it as “Completed” or “Past”, instead of “Scheduled”.

Show a “Leave a Review” button or link under completed appointments.

On click, allow users to rate and optionally write feedback about the session.

Suggested Implementation:

Update appointment status logic based on current timestamp vs. appointment time.

Add a review modal/form (simple text + rating stars) for eligible appointments.

issues.title: Restrict Actions on Past Appointments
issues.body:
Disable Cancel/Reschedule Options for Past Appointments

Currently, users are able to cancel or reschedule appointments that have already passed, both from:

The calendar view, and

The My Appointments section.

This creates confusion and affects the accuracy of appointment tracking.

Expected Behavior:

Once an appointment's date + time is in the past:

The "Cancel" and "Reschedule" buttons should be disabled or hidden.

A status like Completed or Expired should be displayed instead.

Suggested Implementation:

On render of appointments, check if current Date.now() is > appointmentDateTime.

Apply conditional rendering to hide or disable actions accordingly.

issues.title: Improve Add Service Modal — Auto-Generate Time Slots & Validation
issues.body:
The "Add New Service" modal should allow users to easily input service availability without typing each slot manually.

Instead of entering each time slot manually, users should just:

Enter slot duration (e.g., 30 minutes)

Enter start time (e.g., 10:00 AM)

Enter end time (e.g., 5:00 PM)

The UI should then auto-generate slots in this format:
[10:00 AM, 10:30 AM, 11:00 AM, ..., 4:30 PM]

✨ Features & Tasks
🔧 Functionality to Add:
 Replace the current “Add Slot” text field with:

A "Start Time" input (time picker or HH:MM format)

An "End Time" input

A "Slot Duration" dropdown or number input (in minutes)

 On clicking “Generate Slots”, auto-fill the list of time slots from start to end time based on duration.

 Display the generated slots below for preview (with option to remove/edit if needed).

✅ Form Validations:
 All fields (Service name, Description, Price, Duration, etc.) must be required.

 Validate that duration is a positive number, and start < end time.

 Validate contact (email/phone) is in correct format.

 If "Image URL" is provided, validate it's a valid URL.

 At least 1 generated time slot must exist before submission.

 Display proper success/error messages on submit.

🎨 UI Improvements:
 Modal should remain fully responsive on small screens.

 Add a reset/clear button to re-enter the time range if needed.

issues.title: Improve Service Card UI & Add Demo Data
issues.body:
The UI of the service cards under each category needs refinement to align with the Indian audience and enhance user experience.

Tasks:
Replace the dollar symbol $ with the Indian Rupee symbol ₹ in all price fields.

Add demo ratings (e.g., 4.3 stars with 12 reviews) to each card for better visual feedback.

Fix the "Know More" button:

It opens detailed info, but currently does not close on second click — this toggle behavior needs fixing.

Ensure that all cards have dummy reviews, ratings, and complete demo data filled properly.

Expected Outcome:
All service cards should:

Display price with ₹.

Show a consistent dummy rating and review count.

Open/close the detailed "Know More" section properly.

Look uniform and visually complete across categories.

issues.title: Add “My Services” Page in Navbar for Service Providers
issues.body:
Enable service providers to manage their offerings through a dedicated dashboard.

🔧 Tasks:
Add a "My Services" link to the Navbar (visible only if user has added any service).

On this page, allow providers to:

View list of their added services.

Track who booked each appointment and at what time.

Update:

Service availability

Total slots or remaining seats

Edit/delete service options

Show appointment-related data only to the owner of that service.

🛡️ Access Control:

Only authenticated users who added services should see this page.

Use role check or conditional rendering based on their service submission.

issues.title: Backend Integration for Add Services & Global Service Display
issues.body:
Connect Add Services Form to Backend + Show Services Publicly

Currently, added services are not integrated with the backend properly or are visible only on the user’s profile. This issue focuses on fixing that.

🔧 Tasks:
Connect the Add Services form to the backend API.

Ensure services are stored under the correct category.

Show newly added services in:

The correct Service Category page.

All profiles, not just the one who created it.

Validate that all form fields (name, price, time, description, etc.) are submitted and stored correctly.

Audit and fix any bugs in existing service APIs.

issues.title: Revamp UI for Add Services Form & Category Pages
issues.body:
Redesign Add Services Form and Service Category Pages (UI Enhancements)
Tasks:
Redesign the Add Services form to have a cleaner and more modern UI (spacing, styling, alignment).

Fix Beauty & Personal Care page:

Replace dollar pricing with ₹ INR.

Add realistic duration (not fake times like 5 hours for a haircut).

Add placeholder reviews or proper design for when no reviews are available.

Apply the same fixes to other service categories.

Autofill category in the form based on current section:

Example: If you're on Healthcare, clicking "Add Service" should pre-fill the category as “Healthcare”.

issues.title: implementing consistent and proper service fields
Add Calendar View for Appointments
issues.body:
Enhance the "My Appointments" page by replacing the basic list with a calendar-based appointment interface that allows users to visually track and interact with their bookings. This will significantly improve the usability and intuitiveness of the platform.

📚 Requirements:
Use a Calendar Library

Integrate [react-calendar](https://github.com/wojtekmaj/react-calendar) or @fullcalendar/react.

Display appointments on their respective dates using dot indicators, color tags, or event cards.

Highlight Appointment Dates

Mark dates that have one or more appointments booked.

Hovering over or clicking on a date should show a list of those appointments.

Appointment Modal (Click Event)

On clicking a booked date:

Show popup/modal with:

Appointment title

Time slot

Provider/service name

Status (e.g., upcoming, completed)

Allow option for cancel appointment (bonus)

Date Navigation

Users should be able to scroll across weeks/months.

Default calendar should open on the current month and highlight today's date.

Mobile Responsive UI

Ensure calendar collapses well on mobile devices.

Consider a week view or agenda view for small screens.

Display Today’s Appointments

Add a “📍 Today” button to quickly jump to current day’s appointments.

Show any appointments scheduled for today in a sidebar or modal.

Empty State Handling

Show friendly message when no appointments are booked yet.

Suggest “Book Your First Appointment” with a call-to-action button.

Add reschedule option in modal (opens reschedule form)

Filter by service category

Use different colors/tags for services (e.g., blue for healthcare, pink for beauty)

issues.title: Add Feedback & Rating System for Services
issues.body:
Allow users to leave ratings and comments after they complete an appointment. This helps build trust for services and gives providers valuable feedback.

📌 What Needs to Be Done
1. Create a Review Model (Backend)
In your backend (server/src/models/Review.js), create a new Mongoose model like this:

serviceId: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId,  // Which service was reviewed
userId: mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId,     // Who gave the review
rating: Number,                             // 1 to 5 stars
comment: String,                            // Optional comment
timestamp: Date                             // When the review was posted

2. Create API Routes (Backend)
POST /reviews/add → Add a new review (only for logged-in users)

GET /reviews/service/:id → Get all reviews for a specific service

3. Update the UI (Frontend)
On each service page (or after appointment), show a form:

⭐ Star rating input (1–5)

📝 Comment box

✅ Submit button

After submission, show a thank you message or toast.

4. Display Average Ratings
On the service card, display the average star rating and number of reviews (e.g., ⭐ 4.6 (12 Reviews))

issues.title: Expand Register Form and Create User Profile Dashboard
issues.body:
🎯 Objective:
Enhance the current registration process to collect more meaningful user data and build a User Profile Dashboard to display and later allow editing of these details.

📌 Register Form – Additional Fields to Collect:
👤 Full Name

📞 Phone Number

🏠 Address

🎂 Date of Birth (optional)

🧑‍💼 Gender (dropdown)

✉️ Email (already exists)

🔒 Password (already exists)

📍 Update the frontend Register.jsx to include these fields with validation
📍 Ensure the backend /auth/register route and MongoDB User schema is updated to store these values

🧱 Backend Tasks:
Update the User model (server/src/models/User.js)

Update /auth/register route to save this data

Create a new route:

GET /user/profile  # To fetch user data
PUT /user/profile  # To allow user to update their profile
✅ Use JWT token to verify and identify user

🎨 Frontend Tasks:
Update Register.jsx to add fields with validation

Create a new page: UserProfile.jsx

Shows user info pulled from /user/profile

Fields should be editable

Add “Update Profile” button

Add Toast for success/error

🚀 End Result:
Registration will feel more complete and professional

Every user will have their personal dashboard

Sets the foundation for future personalization, settings, and analytics

issues.title: Add My Appointments Dashboard
issues.body:
Users should have a dedicated /my-appointments page where they can view:

Upcoming appointments

Past appointments

Appointment details

Cancel/Reschedule button (future scope)

📋 Display fields:

Service Name

Date and Time

Location/Address

Status: Confirmed, Completed, Cancelled

issues.title: Appointment Booking Notification System
issues.body:
mplement an automated notification system to remind users about their booked appointments at multiple intervals.

📩 User should receive notifications:

24 hours before

12 hours before

6 hours before

1 hour before

30 minutes before

5 minutes before

At the time of appointment

🧠 Expected Behavior:

These reminders should be sent via email (and later possibly SMS/Push)

Use a background job or cron scheduler (e.g., node-cron, Agenda, or hosted services like Firebase Cloud Functions)

🛠️ Backend Tasks:

Store booking time in UTC

Create a background script to:

Scan for upcoming appointments every 10–15 minutes

Calculate time difference

Trigger email notifications

Integrate with a mail service: Nodemailer + Gmail, Mailgun, or SendGrid

📥 User Perspective:

While booking, a message like:
"You’ll receive timely reminders before your appointment." should be shown

Appointments must appear on /my-appointments page with their time + status

issues.title: Add Service Submission Form (Dynamic Service Listing)
issues.body:
Allow service providers to dynamically add new services to categories (e.g., Healthcare, Beauty). A new “Add Service” button should be shown in every service category page.

🧩 Feature Requirements:

Add a button: + Add a Service visible on category pages (bottom or top right)

On click → open a form modal/page with fields:

Service Name

Category (dropdown)

Description

Company/Brand name

Address + City

Availability Time Slots (select multiple)

Contact email/phone

Optional image/logo upload

On submit → save to backend (POST /services)

That service should appear in the list of services under that category

🛠️ Backend Tasks:

Create services schema in MongoDB

Add POST /services route to save service data

Add GET /services/:category to fetch by category

🧪 Frontend Tasks:

ServiceForm.jsx (modal/component)

Validate form inputs

Show alert/Toast after successful submission

Reload category view to show the new service

issues.title: Create User Profile Component
issues.body:
Create a User Profile Component that displays the logged-in user's information and allows future profile settings or updates.

✅ Goals:
 Create a new React component UserProfile.jsx

 Display user's:

Email (fetched from JWT or backend API)

Registration date

 Style the component using CSS modules or styled-components

 Add a "Logout" button inside this component

 (Optional) Add a placeholder profile picture

 Add navigation so authenticated users can access /profile

🧠 Notes:
Make sure user info is only accessible when JWT token exists.

You can decode the token using jwt-decode or fetch profile data from backend (GET /auth/me)

This is a placeholder for future features like profile picture, name, etc.

issues.title: Enable Google Sign-In Authentication
issues.body:
Implement Google Sign-In using OAuth2 to allow users to authenticate via their Google account in addition to traditional email/password.

✅ Goals:
 Add Google Login button on the login page.

 Integrate Google OAuth2.0 flow using a library like:

react-oauth/google (frontend)

passport-google-oauth20 or manual token verification with Google API (backend)

 After successful login, issue a JWT token and store the user in MongoDB (if not already).

 Auto-fill name/email from Google profile in the UI (optional).

🧠 Notes:
You'll need to create OAuth credentials from the Google Cloud Console and add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID to your .env.

Handle both new user registration and existing user login from Google.

issues.title: Apply basic styling to auth pages
issues.body: Use simple CSS or Tailwind for cleaner UI on Login/Register.

issues.title: Create Wireframes
issues.body:
Design the basic wireframes (low-fidelity sketches) for the FixTime platform. This will help the team and contributors visualize how the final product will look and function before diving deeper into UI development.

You can create the wireframes using any of the following tools:

Figma (preferred for digital collaboration)

Balsamiq

Pen & Paper (draw on copy and upload a clear picture)

Whimsical / Excalidraw / Canva

📌 What should be included in the wireframes:

Landing Page / Home Page

Header / Navbar

Hero Section (FixTime tagline and CTA button)

List of available services or popular providers

Auth Page

Register & Login toggle design

Input fields (Email, Password, Confirm Password)

Success/Error message layout

User Dashboard (after login)

Appointment Booking section

List of upcoming bookings

Profile access

Service Provider Dashboard (Optional but appreciated)

Time slot management

View booked appointments

Profile and availability

📂 Delivery:

You can submit your wireframes as image uploads (JPG/PNG) or PDFs.

If using Figma or an online tool, please share the public link in a comment under this issue.

issues.title: GET /appointments/my route
issues.body: Return all appointments for the logged-in user.

issues.title: POST /appointments/book route
issues.body: Backend route to let a user book an appointment.

issues.title: Create Appointment Schema
issues.body: Add MongoDB schema for appointments: user, provider, date, timeSlot.

issues.title: Add error handling middleware
issues.body:
To make the backend more reliable and developer-friendly, we need to implement a centralized error-handling middleware in the Express server. This ensures that:

All errors from routes or async operations are captured in one place

The API responds with clean, consistent error messages

Debugging becomes easier during development

🛠️ Tasks:
 Create a new middleware/errorHandler.js file

 Define a function that takes (err, req, res, next) and returns a JSON error response

 Add this middleware at the bottom of the middleware stack in server/src/index.js

 In routes (e.g., auth.js, appointments.js), replace try-catch with next(err) to forward errors

 Ensure validation or DB errors are passed to this middleware

✅ Expected Result:
API errors return consistent response structure

Errors don’t crash the server

Easier logging/debugging

issues.title: Add validation for empty fields (register/login)
issues.body: Prevent empty input in backend and return custom error messages.

issues.title: Auto-Redirect If Logged In
issues.body: If the user is already logged in and visits /login or /register, automatically redirect them to home.

issues.title: Add Password Visibility Toggle
issues.body: Add an 👁️ icon in login/register forms to toggle password visibility.

issues.title: Show Toast Alerts for Success/Error (Login/Register/Booking)
issues.body:
Use a library like react-toastify or a custom Toast component to:

Show success on login/register

Show error if credentials are wrong

Reusable for all future pages

issues.title: Create a Responsive Appointment Card Component
issues.body: Build a reusable appointment card UI component that will be used in the user dashboard to display booked appointments. 📦 The Appointment Card should include: ✅ User Email 📅 Selected Date ⏰ Selected Time Slot 🟢 Status Badge – showing: Upcoming (if the time has not yet passed) Completed (if the time is in the past) Reason for Visit 🖼️ Design Reference: Refer to the current appointment booking form UI (see attached screenshot) for consistency. 🔁 Behavior Logic: 🕒 If a time slot has already passed, that time should not be clickable/selectable during booking. 🚫 Once booked, the same slot should not be available for another appointment. 📌 The card component should dynamically reflect the correct status (Upcoming / Completed) based on the current time and appointment date-time. 📱 Responsiveness: The card must be fully responsive and look good on both desktop and mobile screens. 📎 Note: Once completed, this card will be integrated into the user dashboard where all upcoming and past appointments will be shown in a list format.

issues.title: Add Dark Mode Toggle
issues.body:
Add a dark mode/light mode switch to the Navbar that toggles a CSS class or Tailwind theme.
Store the preference in localStorage so it persists on reload.

issues.title: Create Appointment Booking UI (static)
issues.body: Add a page with date picker + time slot dropdown (functionality not required yet).

issues.title: Setup Routing with React Router DOM
issues.body:
Set up routing in the React app using React Router DOM. Define paths for all key pages:

/ → Auth Page

/appointments → Appointment Booking Page

Also add a basic navigation bar to switch between pages, and include a 404 Not Found route for invalid URLs.

issues.title: Basic Navbar Component
issues.body: Build a responsive Navbar with links to Home, Login, Register (based on login state).

issues.title: Implement Logout Functionality
issues.body: Add a logout button to clear auth token and redirect to login.

issues.title: Create Protected Home Page
issues.body:
Design and implement a protected home page that is only accessible to authenticated users (based on JWT token in localStorage).

Requirements:

🔐 Authentication:
Redirect to /auth if token is missing or invalid

Show success toast when the user successfully logs in

🧭 Navbar:
Home

Book Appointment

Services

Profile

Logout

🏠 Home Page:
Display a welcome message (with user’s email or name if available)

Add placeholder widgets/sections for:

Upcoming appointments

Recently booked

General dashboard area

🧰 Services Section:
Display a list of available services (can be static for now)

Allow users to search services by keyword

Style results cleanly with cards or a grid view

Add a filter dropdown (optional) by category

🔓 Logout Functionality:
Clear token from localStorage

Redirect user to /auth with a toast

issues.title: Show success/error messages on login/register
issues.body: Display toast or alert based on response (e.g., invalid credentials).

issues.title: Connect Login Form to Backend API
issues.body: Use Axios to send POST request to /auth/login.

issues.title: Connect Register Form to Backend API
issues.body: Use Axios to send POST request to /auth/register.

issues.title: Create Login Form UI
issues.body: Design the login form with Email and Password fields.
